summary: Recent updates to the Media Source Extensions (MSE) in Firefox have introduced important testing capabilities for HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding), a video compression standard that allows for high-quality streaming with lower data usage. The addition of a CMAF (Common Media Application Format) test without SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) enhances the ability to evaluate how well the browser handles HEVC encoded content. These improvements ensure that Firefox can better support modern video formats, which is essential for delivering high-quality streaming experiences on the web. **This is significant as it directly impacts how efficiently users can enjoy high-definition video content online.**
